steps.title: Identify the evidence for independence
steps.content: The passage notes: “a difference that persisted even after fruited bodies were detached“—this shows the temperature difference between fruiting and nonfruiting sites of the mycelium exists independent of fruited body presence.

steps.title: Match to the choices
steps.content:
Choice A states that thermoregulation variation in the mycelium occurs independent of fruited bodies—and the persistence of the temperature difference after detachment proves exactly this.

The other three either misstate the temperature relationships, introduce unmentioned details, or make unsupported comparisons.

That matches choice A — A is correct.
